name: cargo-fuzz
pricing_notes: Fully open source; uses libFuzzer built into LLVM/Rust toolchain

auth.notes: Cargo subcommand; no auth required; requires nightly Rust toolchain

performance.notes: Native Rust performance; millions of iterations per second; significantly faster than JVM/Python fuzzers

agent_readiness.notes: Official Rust fuzzing tool using libFuzzer via LLVM sanitizers. Simple cargo subcommand interface (cargo fuzz run <target>). Structured input with `arbitrary` crate. Corpus management. Integrated with OSS-Fuzz. Finds memory issues (via AddressSanitizer), panics, and crashes. Requires nightly Rust.

gotchas: Requires nightly Rust toolchain (not stable Rust)
libFuzzer coverage-guided but different from AFL++ (different strengths)
Fuzz targets must be pure functions with no global state
Corpus management and reproduction can be complex for beginners
best_when: Rust parsers, deserializers, and format handlers needing crash detection
OSS-Fuzz continuous fuzzing for open source Rust crates
Finding panics and undefined behavior in Rust code
avoid_when: Non-Rust code (use Atheris for Python, Jazzer for Java)
Application-level testing (designed for library API fuzzing)
summary: cargo-fuzz is the official Rust fuzzing tool using libFuzzer via LLVM sanitizers. Simple cargo subcommand interface. The `arbitrary` crate enables structured fuzzing of complex types. Native performance (millions of iterations/second). Requires nightly Rust. Integrated with OSS-Fuzz for continuous fuzzing. Standard choice for fuzzing Rust parsers, cryptography libraries, and protocol implementations.
